Colorado · State Licensing Reference

Dietitian
Licensed Dietitian (LD) — Effective September 1, 2026 Guide

Issued by the State Board of Dietetics and Nutrition (New) under the Colorado Department of Regulatory Agencies (DORA), Division of Professions and Occupations. Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
~$239.50 (Estimated)
~$200 initial registration fee + $39.50 CBI background check (Estimated for FY 2026-27)
Renewal
~$125–$180 (Estimated)
Annual (Estimated) + 75 CPE hours per 5-year CDR cycle
Processing Time
TBD (New program launching Sept 2026)
Subject to board volume
License Duration
1 Year (Estimated)
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Fax: (303) 894-7693

Credential Categories

Colorado Dietitian License Types

Licensed Dietitian (LD)

Starting September 1, 2026, Colorado will require a state license to practice or offer medical nutrition therapy. Licensed Dietitians must hold current CDR registration or meet equivalent examination and education requirements.

Licensed Nutritionist (LN)

Colorado's new law also creates a Licensed Nutritionist pathway, which will have a separate examination option (with a waiver period until September 1, 2028 for qualifying candidates).

Application Process

How to Apply for a Colorado Licensed Dietitian (LD) — Effective September 1, 2026

Complete Education & Supervised Practice

Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete at least 1,000 hours of supervised practice in clinical, community, or foodservice settings.

Pass the CDR Examination (or Provide Active RD Status)

Successfully pass the CDR Registration Examination for Dietitians and obtain active registration, OR provide proof of holding valid, current CDR registration.

Complete CBI Background Check

Submit to a Colorado Bureau of Investigation (CBI) fingerprint-based background check ($39.50 fee), required at initial registration.

File Application with DORA

Submit the application to the Director of the Division of Professions and Occupations with proof of education, supervised practice, examination/CDR status, and the estimated $200 registration fee.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • CDR Registration VerificationProof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • CBI Background Check FingerprintsColorado Bureau of Investigation background check, processed at a DORA-approved provider.
  • Proof of Supervised PracticeVerification of at least 1,000 hours of supervised practice from an ACEND-accredited program.
Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Provisional Rules

Colorado's new Board of Dietetics and Nutrition may establish provisions for temporary practice during the transition period. Check the DORA website as regulations are finalized.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Details on reciprocity or endorsement will be established by the new Board of Dietetics and Nutrition under DORA regulations. Active CDR registration will serve as the primary qualification for state licensure.

Frequently Asked Questions

Colorado Dietitian FAQ

When does Colorado's dietitian license requirement take effect?

The new licensure requirement takes effect on September 1, 2026. After this date, no person may practice or offer medical nutrition therapy without a state license.

How much will a Colorado dietitian license cost?

The estimated initial registration fee is approximately $200 for FY 2026-27, plus a $39.50 CBI background check. Annual renewal is estimated between $125 and $180.

Do I need a license before September 1, 2026?

No. Before September 1, 2026, Colorado does not mandate state licensure. The CDR credential (RD/RDN) remains the recognized standard until the new law takes effect.
